generatedSentence: Lancejack Kael, once a formidable 'Blocker' of space raiders, was chilling with a mojito, observing the dusty environment of the south sector from his coconut-shaded hideout. His keyboard sat closed, a symbol of his forgone intelligence work. He had a strong inkling that the notorious Baron Von Con, a sirloin-gorging troublemaker, was tweaking the regional election results, creating a fatal future for every taxpayer. The door to justice felt closed, a matter of immense volume and vexing complexity. The chancellor's orders were clear: the belligerent Von Con's hijack of finances had reached a critical juncture, demanding an amazing response. Kael, feeling burdened by the lunatics Von Con controlled, realized he couldn't just enjoy a peaceful lunchbreak. His enthusiasm for justice, ignited by a new user handbook, began to multiply. He saw the gradient of corruption, an ornamental façade over the orthodox system, and knew his old reasoning for fighting was still upright, despite the rust on his old gear. He'd need to strategize. Donning his old suit, now a bit unkempt but still cinched, Kael felt a surge of confidence. He imagined himself a modern wizard, performing karate moves with amazing velocity, a firehose of righteous fury. He carried no narcotics, just tasers, as he jumped into the fray, a symbol of light against the encroaching darkness. He knew this wouldn't be a joke; the violence and visceral nature of Von Con's empire, like crocodiles in a murky ocean, required more than just a party. He needed to impress and make a difference, avoiding the con that had entrapped so many. In a climactic bunker showdown, Kael confronted Von Con's barrack of henchmen, whose faces were splattered with ketchup and corn from a recent pastry jam. He unleashed a series of precise attacks, making them all feel the sting of justice. Von Con, a true stinker, was caught dipping into the hydrogen fuel cells, attempting to randomize his escape. Kael's swift judgement and peekaboo tactics secured the victory, not just for the unionized mule drivers, but for the collective glory and coolness of all taxpayers. It was a victory for the kitty of justice, bringing back the dopamine of freedom, above the cactus and raindrop of tyranny, to the grateful masses, reminding them of the true legends of their world.
panelSentences: Lancejack Kael, once a formidable 'Blocker' of space raiders, was chilling with a mojito, observing the dusty environment of the south sector from his coconut-shaded hideout. His keyboard sat closed, a symbol of his forgone intelligence work. He had a strong inkling that the notorious Baron Von Con, a sirloin-gorging troublemaker, was tweaking the regional election results, creating a fatal future for every taxpayer. The door to justice felt closed, a matter of immense volume and vexing complexity.

The chancellor's orders were clear: the belligerent Von Con's hijack of finances had reached a critical juncture, demanding an amazing response. Kael, feeling burdened by the lunatics Von Con controlled, realized he couldn't just enjoy a peaceful lunchbreak. His enthusiasm for justice, ignited by a new user handbook, began to multiply. He saw the gradient of corruption, an ornamental façade over the orthodox system, and knew his old reasoning for fighting was still upright, despite the rust on his old gear. He'd need to strategize.

Donning his old suit, now a bit unkempt but still cinched, Kael felt a surge of confidence. He imagined himself a modern wizard, performing karate moves with amazing velocity, a firehose of righteous fury. He carried no narcotics, just tasers, as he jumped into the fray, a symbol of light against the encroaching darkness. He knew this wouldn't be a joke; the violence and visceral nature of Von Con's empire, like crocodiles in a murky ocean, required more than just a party. He needed to impress and make a difference, avoiding the con that had entrapped so many.

In a climactic bunker showdown, Kael confronted Von Con's barrack of henchmen, whose faces were splattered with ketchup and corn from a recent pastry jam. He unleashed a series of precise attacks, making them all feel the sting of justice. Von Con, a true stinker, was caught dipping into the hydrogen fuel cells, attempting to randomize his escape. Kael's swift judgement and peekaboo tactics secured the victory, not just for the unionized mule drivers, but for the collective glory and coolness of all taxpayers. It was a victory for the kitty of justice, bringing back the dopamine of freedom, above the cactus and raindrop of tyranny, to the grateful masses, reminding them of the true legends of their world.
rawPanelSentences: Lancejack Kael, once a formidable 'Blocker' of space raiders, was chilling with a **mojito**, observing the **dusty** **environment** of the **south** sector from his **coconut**-shaded hideout. His **keyboard** sat **closed**, a symbol of his **forgone** intelligence work. He had a strong **inkling** that the notorious **Baron** Von **Con**, a **sirloin**-gorging **troublemaker**, was **tweaking** the regional **election** results, creating a **fatal** future for every **taxpayer**. The **door** to justice felt **closed**, a matter of immense **volume** and **vexing** complexity.

The **chancellor**'s **orders** were clear: the **belligerent** Von Con's **hijack** of **finances** had reached a critical **juncture**, demanding an **amazing** response. Kael, feeling **burdened** by the **lunatics** Von Con controlled, realized he couldn't just enjoy a peaceful **lunchbreak**. His **enthusiasm** for justice, ignited by a new **user** handbook, began to **multiply**. He saw the **gradient** of corruption, an **ornamental** façade over the **orthodox** system, and knew his old **reasoning** for fighting was still upright, despite the **rust** on his old gear. He'd need to **strategize**.

Donning his old suit, now a bit **unkempt** but still **cinched**, Kael felt a surge of **confidence**. He imagined himself a modern **wizard**, performing **karate** moves with amazing **velocity**, a **firehose** of righteous fury. He carried no **narcotics**, just tasers, as he **jumped** into the fray, a symbol of **light** against the encroaching **darkness**. He knew this wouldn't be a **joke**; the **violence** and visceral nature of Von Con's empire, like **crocodiles** in a murky **ocean**, required more than just a **party**. He needed to **impress** and make a difference, avoiding the **con** that had entrapped so many.

In a climactic **bunker** showdown, Kael confronted Von Con's **barrack** of henchmen, whose faces were splattered with **ketchup** and **corn** from a recent **pastry** **jam**. He unleashed a series of precise attacks, making them all feel the sting of justice. Von Con, a true **stinker**, was caught **dipping** into the **hydrogen** fuel cells, attempting to **randomize** his escape. Kael's swift **judgement** and **peekaboo** tactics secured the victory, not just for the **unionized** **mule** drivers, but for the collective **glory** and **coolness** of all taxpayers. It was a victory for the **kitty** of justice, bringing back the **dopamine** of freedom, **above** the **cactus** and **raindrop** of tyranny, to the grateful masses, reminding them of the true **legends** of their world.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a grizzled cyber-noir detective wearing a trench coat made of iridescent black fabric and glowing circuit patterns, with a single bright blue optical implant. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, Kael, sits on a dusty, metallic balcony, sipping a futuristic mojito, observing a neon-lit, sprawling cityscape at dusk, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Kael in a dimly lit, high-tech control room, hunched over a glowing holographic keyboard, a worried expression on his face as data streams around him, medium close-up. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Kael, now in a dynamic action pose, mid-air with tasers drawn, leaping over a pile of futuristic debris towards a group of armored henchmen, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael stands triumphantly over a defeated, cowering Baron Von Con in a brightly lit, circular bunker, one hand on his hip, the other holding a deactivated taser, triumphant mid-shot. Bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: A tomato-vinegar-based sauce, sometimes containing spices, onion or garlic, and (especially in the US) sweeteners.
Such a sauce more generally (not necessarily based on tomatoes).
definitions.example: fish ketchup; fruit ketchup; mushroom ketchup

definitions.definition: To cover with ketchup.
(etc.) A set of keys used to operate a typewriter, computer etc.
A component of many instruments including the piano, organ, and harpsichord consisting of usually black and white keys that cause different tones to be produced when struck.
A device with keys of a musical keyboard, used to control electronic sound-producing devices which may be built into or separate from the keyboard device.
To type on a computer keyboard.
definitions.example: Keyboarding is the part of this job I hate the most.

definitions.definition: To lower the temperature of something; to cool
definitions.example: Chill before serving.

definitions.definition: To become cold
definitions.example: In the wind he chilled quickly.

definitions.definition: To harden a metal surface by sudden cooling
To become hard by rapid cooling
To relax, lie back
definitions.example: Chill, man, we've got a whole week to do it; no sense in getting worked up.

definitions.definition: To "hang", hang out; to spend time with another person or group. Also chill out.
definitions.example: Hey, we should chill this weekend.

definitions.definition: To smoke marijuana
definitions.example: On Friday night do you wanna chill?

definitions.definition: To discourage, depress
definitions.example: Censorship chills public discourse.

definitions.definition: The act by which something is chilled.
Becoming cold.
Causing cold.
Causing mild fear.
definitions.example: It was a chilling story, but the children enjoyed it.

definitions.definition: A fruit of the coconut palm (not a true nut), Cocos nucifera, having a fibrous husk surrounding a large seed.
A hard-shelled seed of this fruit, having white flesh and a fluid-filled central cavity.
The edible white flesh of this fruit.
The coconut palm.
A Hispanic or dark-skinned person who acts “white” (Caucasian), alluding to the fact that a coconut is brown on the outside and white on the inside.
A black person who thinks or acts "white" (European).
A Pacific islander.
A female breast.
The human head (often used in cricket broadcasts when a ball hits or nearly hits a batsman on the head).
(physical) To remove a gap.
(social) To finish, to terminate.
To come or gather around; to enclose; to encompass; to confine.
To have a vector sum of 0; that is, to form a closed polygon.
Sealed, made inaccessible or impassable; not open.
(of a store or business) Not operating or conducting trade.
Not public.
definitions.example: closed source  a closed committee

definitions.definition: (of a set) Having an open complement.
(of a set) Such that its image under the specified operation is contained in it.
definitions.example: The set of integers is closed under addition: \forall x,y\in\mathbb{Z}\,x+y\in\mathbb{Z}.

definitions.definition: (of a formula) Lacking a free variable.
(of a walk) Whose first and last vertices are the same, forming a closed loop.
Formed by closing the mouth and nose passages completely, like the consonants /t/, /d/ and /p/.
Having the sound cut off sharply by a following consonant, like the /ɪ/ in pin.
Self-assurance.
A feeling of certainty; firm trust or belief; faith.
Information held in secret.
Boldness; presumption.
To study or examine carefully, especially in order to gain knowledge of; to learn, or learn by heart.
To know, understand, acknowledge.
The male ruler of a barony.
A male member of the lowest rank of English nobility (the equivalent rank in Scotland is lord).
A particular cut of beef, made up of a double sirloin.
A person of great power in society, especially in business and politics.
A prisoner who gains power and influence by lending or selling tobacco.
A husband.
definitions.example: baron and feme: husband and wife

definitions.definition: Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Euthalia.
Agent noun of block; something that blocks something else.
A cut of beef from the lower part of the back, where the last ribs are (called rump in UK English).
A cut of beef from the middle of the back (corresponding to short loin and partly rib in US English).
A lance-corporal or lance-bombadier.
An insane person.
The imagination.
An image or representation of anything formed in the mind.
An opinion or notion formed without much reflection.
A whim.
definitions.example: I had a fancy to learn to play the flute.

definitions.definition: Love or amorous attachment.
definitions.example: He took a fancy to her.

definitions.definition: The object of inclination or liking.
Any sport or hobby pursued by a group.
definitions.example: Trainspotting is the fancy of a special lot.

definitions.definition: The enthusiasts of such a pursuit.
definitions.example: He fell out of favor with the boxing fancy after the incident.

definitions.definition: A diamond with a distinctive colour.
That which pleases or entertains the taste or caprice without much use or value.
A sort of love song or light impromptu ballad.
In the game of jacks, a style of play involving additional actions (contrasted with plainsies).
Decorative.
definitions.example: This is a fancy shawl.

definitions.definition: Of a superior grade.
definitions.example: This box contains bottles of the fancy grade of jelly.

definitions.definition: Executed with skill.
definitions.example: He initiated the game winning play with a fancy, deked saucer pass to the winger.

definitions.definition: Unnecessarily complicated.
definitions.example: I'm not keen on him and his fancy ideas.

definitions.definition: Extravagant; above real value.
In a fancy manner; fancily.
A fatality; an event that leads to death.
A fatal error; a failure that causes a program to terminate.
Proceeding from, or appointed by, fate or destiny.
Foreboding death or great disaster.
Causing death or destruction.
definitions.example: a fatal wound; a fatal disease; that fatal day; a fatal mistake

definitions.definition: Causing a sudden end to the running of a program.
definitions.example: a fatal error; a fatal exception

definitions.definition: The surroundings of, and influences on, a particular item of interest.
The natural world or ecosystem.
All the elements that affect a system or its inputs and outputs.
A particular political or social setting, arena or condition.
The software and/or hardware existing on any particular computer system.
definitions.example: That program uses the Microsoft Windows environment.

definitions.definition: The environment of a function at a point during the execution of a program is the set of identifiers in the function's scope and their bindings at that point.
The set of variables and their values in a namespace that an operating system associates with a process.
Possession by a god; divine inspiration or frenzy.
Intensity of feeling; excited interest or eagerness.
Something in which one is keenly interested.
One who engages in a raid; a plunderer.
A person who takes or attempts to take control of a firm against the will of current management by purchasing a controlling interest of stock and acquiring proxies.
A special forces operative; a commando.
A warship which is light, maneuverable, and fast-moving.
A person who uncovers evidence of improper behavior within governmental or private organizations.
To deduce or come to a conclusion by being rational
To perform a process of deduction or of induction, in order to convince or to confute; to argue.
To converse; to compare opinions.
To arrange and present the reasons for or against; to examine or discuss by arguments; to debate or discuss.
definitions.example: I reasoned the matter with my friend.

definitions.definition: To support with reasons, as a request.
To persuade by reasoning or argument.
definitions.example: to reason one into a belief; to reason one out of his plan

definitions.definition: (with down) To overcome or conquer by adducing reasons.
definitions.example: to reason down a passion

definitions.definition: (usually with out) To find by logical process; to explain or justify by reason or argument.
definitions.example: to reason out the causes of the librations of the moon

definitions.definition: The deduction of inferences or interpretations from premises; abstract thought; ratiocination.
A Rastafari meeting held for the purposes of chanting, prayer and discussion.
A region of space that contains no matter.
(plural only "vacuums") A vacuum cleaner.
The condition of rarefaction, or reduction of pressure below that of the atmosphere, in a vessel, such as the condenser of a steam engine, which is nearly exhausted of air or steam, etc.
definitions.example: a vacuum of 26 inches of mercury, or 13 pounds per square inch

definitions.definition: A spacetime having tensors of zero magnitude
To clean (something) with a vacuum cleaner.
To use a vacuum cleaner.
To optimise a database or database table by physically removing deleted tuples.
A Cuban cocktail, generally made from rum, lime, sugar, mint, etc.
An act or instance of multiplying.
To increase the amount, degree or number of (something).
(with by) To perform multiplication on (a number).
definitions.example: when you multiply 3 by 7, you get 21;  he multiplied several numbers

definitions.definition: To grow in number.
To breed or propagate.
To perform multiplication.
definitions.example: He had been multiplying, but it occurred to him he needed to resolve the exponents, first.

definitions.definition: To be a factor in a multiplication with (another factor).
One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer/client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
A person who uses drugs, especially illegal drugs.
A person who uses a computer or a computing network, especially a person who has received a user account.
An exploiter, an abuser (a person who "uses" people, that is treats and regards people unfairly, selfishly and/or unethically).

In land law, meaning either 1. or 2. above or use. Usually in singular form to mean use wherever there is assiduous re-use of precedents and aloof textbooks verbatim. Modern law, guarded against ambiguity, widely disfavors the term.
A musical instrument of the brass family, generally tuned to the key of B-flat; by extension, any type of lip-vibrated aerophone, most often valveless and not chromatic.
definitions.example: The royal herald sounded a trumpet to announce their arrival.

definitions.definition: Someone who plays the trumpet; a trumpeter.
definitions.example: The trumpets were assigned to stand at the rear of the orchestra pit.

definitions.definition: The cry of an elephant, or any similar loud cry.
definitions.example: The large bull gave a basso trumpet as he charged the hunters.

definitions.definition: One who praises, or propagates praise, or is the instrument of propagating it.
A funnel, or short flaring pipe, used as a guide or conductor, as for yarn in a knitting machine.
A kind of traffic interchange involving at least one loop ramp connecting traffic either entering or leaving the terminating expressway with the far lanes of the continuous highway.
A powerful reed stop in organs, having a trumpet-like sound.
To sound loudly, be amplified
definitions.example: The music trumpeted from the speakers, hurting my ears.

definitions.definition: To play the trumpet.
definitions.example: Cedric made a living trumpeting for the change of passersby in the subway.

definitions.definition: Of an elephant, to make its cry.
definitions.example: The circus trainer cracked the whip, signaling the elephant to trumpet.

definitions.definition: To give a loud cry like that of an elephant.
To proclaim loudly; to promote enthusiastically
definitions.example: Andy trumpeted Jane's secret across the school, much to her embarrassment.

definitions.definition: To pinch and pull with a sudden jerk and twist; to twitch.
definitions.example: to tweak the nose.

definitions.definition: To adjust slightly; to fine-tune.
definitions.example: If we tweak the colors towards blue, it will look more natural.

definitions.definition: To twit or tease.
To abuse methamphetamines, especially crystal meth.
To exhibit symptoms of methamphetamine abuse, such as extreme nervousness, compulsiveness, erratic motion, excitability; possibly a blend of twitch and freak.
To exhibit extreme nervousness, evasiveness when confronted by law enforcement or other authority (e.g., customs agents, border patrol, teacher, etc.), mimicking methamphetamine abuse symptoms.
A tweak; a minor modification.
Covered with dust.
Powdery and resembling dust.
Grey in parts.
Ugly, disgusting (a general term of abuse)

A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge. Doors are frequently made of wood or metal. May have a handle to help open and close, a latch to hold the door closed and a lock that ensures the door cannot be opened without the key.
definitions.example: I knocked on the vice president's door

definitions.definition: Any flap, etc. that opens like a door.
definitions.example: the 24 doors in an Advent calendar

definitions.definition: (immigration) An entry point.
A means of approach or access.
definitions.example: Learning is the door to wisdom.

definitions.definition: A barrier.
definitions.example: Keep a door on your anger.

definitions.definition: A software mechanism by which a user can interact with a program running remotely on a bulletin board system. See BBS door.
To cause a collision by opening the door of a vehicle in front of an oncoming cyclist or pedestrian.
To build or place (something) so as to face eastward.

(by extension) To align or place (a person or object) so that his, her, or its east side, north side, etc., is positioned toward the corresponding points of the compass; (specifically) to rotate (a map attached to a plane table) until the line of direction between any two of its points is parallel to the corresponding direction in nature.
To direct towards or point at a particular direction.
definitions.example: The workers oriented all the signs to face the road.

definitions.definition: To determine which direction one is facing.
definitions.example: Let me just orient myself and we can be on our way.

definitions.definition: To familiarize (oneself or someone) with a circumstance or situation.
definitions.example: Give him time to orient himself within the new hierarchy.

definitions.definition: To set the focus of (something) so as to appeal or relate to a certain group.
definitions.example: We will orient our campaign to the youth who are often disinterested.

definitions.definition: To change direction to face a certain way.
Having a specific orientation.
Arrangement, disposition, or sequence.
A position in an arrangement, disposition, or sequence.
The state of being well arranged.
definitions.example: The house is in order; the machinery is out of order.

definitions.definition: Conformity with law or decorum; freedom from disturbance; general tranquillity; public quiet.
definitions.example: to preserve order in a community or an assembly

definitions.definition: A command.
A request for some product or service; a commission to purchase, sell, or supply goods.
A group of religious adherents, especially monks or nuns, set apart within their religion by adherence to a particular rule or set of principles.
definitions.example: St. Ignatius Loyola founded the Jesuit order in 1537.

definitions.definition: An association of knights.
definitions.example: the Order of the Garter, the Order of the Bath.

definitions.definition: Any group of people with common interests.
A decoration, awarded by a government, a dynastic house, or a religious body to an individual, usually for distinguished service to a nation or to humanity.
A rank in the classification of organisms, below class and above family; a taxon at that rank.
definitions.example: Magnolias belong to the order Magnoliales.

definitions.definition: A number of things or persons arranged in a fixed or suitable place, or relative position; a rank; a row; a grade; especially, a rank or class in society; a distinct character, kind, or sort.
definitions.example: talent of a high order

definitions.definition: (chiefly plural) An ecclesiastical grade or rank, as of deacon, priest, or bishop; the office of the Christian ministry.
definitions.example: to take orders, or to take holy orders, that is, to enter some grade of the ministry

definitions.definition: The disposition of a column and its component parts, and of the entablature resting upon it, in classical architecture; hence (since the column and entablature are the characteristic features of classical architecture) a style or manner of architectural design.
The sequence in which a side’s batsmen bat; the batting order.
A power of polynomial function in an electronic circuit’s block, such as a filter, an amplifier, etc.
definitions.example: a 3-stage cascade of a 2nd-order bandpass Butterworth filter

definitions.definition: The overall power of the rate law of a chemical reaction, expressed as a polynomial function of concentrations of reactants and products.
The cardinality, or number of elements in a set, group, or other structure regardable as a set.

(of an element of a group) For given group G and element g ∈ G, the smallest positive natural number n, if it exists, such that (using multiplicative notation), gn = e, where e is the identity element of G; if no such number exists, the element is said to be of infinite order (or sometimes zero order).
The number of vertices in a graph.
A partially ordered set.
The relation on a partially ordered set that determines that it is, in fact, a partially ordered set.
The sum of the exponents on the variables in a monomial, or the highest such among all monomials in a polynomial.
definitions.example: A quadratic polynomial, a x^2 + b x + c, is said to be of order (or degree) 2.

definitions.definition: A written direction to furnish someone with money or property; compare money order, postal order.
To set in some sort of order.
To arrange, set in proper order.
To issue a command to.
definitions.example: He ordered me to leave.

definitions.definition: To request some product or service; to secure by placing an order.
definitions.example: to order groceries

definitions.definition: To admit to holy orders; to ordain; to receive into the ranks of the ministry.
A senior secretary or official with administrative or legal duties, sometimes in charge of some area of government such as finance or justice.
definitions.example: Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster  Lord Chancellor

definitions.definition: The head of the government in some German-speaking countries.
definitions.example: the Austrian Chancellor

definitions.definition: A senior record keeper of a cathedral; a senior legal officer for a bishop or diocese in charge of hearing cases involving ecclesiastical law.
The head of a university, sometimes purely ceremonial.
Short for Chancellor of the Exchequer.
The foreman of a jury.
The chief judge of a court of chancery (that is, one exercising equity jurisdiction).
An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.
An amendment which deletes the contents of a bill and inserts entirely new provisions.
Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
A state or other armed participant in warfare
definitions.example: The UN sent a treaty proposal to the belligerents.

definitions.definition: Engaged in warfare, warring.
Eager to go to war, warlike.
Of or pertaining to war.
(by extension) Aggressively hostile, eager to fight.
Acting violently towards others.
Uncooperative.

Visible electromagnetic radiation. The human eye can typically detect radiation (light) in the wavelength range of about 400 to 750 nanometers. Nearby shorter and longer wavelength ranges, although not visible, are commonly called ultraviolet and infrared light.
definitions.example: As you can see, this spacious dining-room gets a lot of light in the mornings.

definitions.definition: A source of illumination.
definitions.example: Put that light out!

definitions.definition: Spiritual or mental illumination; enlightenment, useful information.
definitions.example: Can you throw any light on this problem?

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Facts; pieces of information; ideas, concepts.
A notable person within a specific field or discipline.
definitions.example: Picasso was one of the leading lights of the cubist movement.

definitions.definition: The manner in which the light strikes a picture; that part of a picture which represents those objects upon which the light is supposed to fall; the more illuminated part of a landscape or other scene; opposed to shade.
A point of view, or aspect from which a concept, person or thing is regarded.
definitions.example: I'm really seeing you in a different light today.

definitions.definition: A flame or something used to create fire.
definitions.example: Hey, buddy, you got a light?

definitions.definition: A firework made by filling a case with a substance which burns brilliantly with a white or coloured flame.
definitions.example: a Bengal light

definitions.definition: A window, or space for a window in architecture.
definitions.example: This facade has eight south-facing lights.

definitions.definition: The series of squares reserved for the answer to a crossword clue.
definitions.example: The average length of a light on a 15×15 grid is 7 or 8.

definitions.definition: A cross-light in a double acrostic or triple acrostic.
Open view; a visible state or condition; public observation; publicity.
The power of perception by vision.
The brightness of the eye or eyes.
A traffic light, or, by extension, an intersection controlled by one or more that will face a traveler who is receiving instructions.
definitions.example: To get to our house, turn right at the third light.

definitions.definition: A period of rest from work for the purpose of eating lunch.
To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are known for their ability to jump high.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.
definitions.example: She is going to jump from the diving board.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.
definitions.example: to jump a stream

definitions.definition: To employ a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.
definitions.example: The sudden sharp sound made me jump.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.
definitions.example: Share prices jumped by 10% after the company announced record profits.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.
definitions.example: I hate it when people jump the queue.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: The hoodlum jumped a woman in the alley.

definitions.definition: To bring (a vessel) so close to the wind that half her upper sails are laid aback.

The act of impressing.

Usually preceded by forms of to give: a slight hint, implication, or suggestion given.
Often preceded by forms of to get or to have: an imprecise idea or slight knowledge of something; a suspicion.
A desire, an inclination.
A hose designed to deliver water to douse a fire, usually much stronger and wider in diameter than a garden hose.

A human penis.

To smuggle (illegal drugs).

Of a function y = f(x) or the graph of such a function, the rate of change of y with respect to x that is, the amount by which y changes for a certain (often unit) change in x equivalently, the inclination to the X axis of the tangent to the curve of the graph.
The rate at which a physical quantity increases or decreases relative to change in a given variable, especially distance.

A gradual change in color. A color gradient; gradation.

While lowered, the flag is said to be “at the dip.” A flag being carried on a staff may be dipped by leaning it forward at an approximate angle of 45 degrees.

definitions.example: 1945: I got into the bus full of taxpayers who were giving some money to a taxpayer who had on his taxpayer's stomach a little box which allowed the other taxpayers to continue their taxpayers' journeys. I noticed in this bus a taxpayer with a long taxpayer's neck and whose taxpayer's head bore a taxpayer's felt hat encircled by a plait the like of which no taxpayer ever wore before. Suddenly the said taxpayer peremptorily addressed a nearby taxpayer, complaining bitterly that he was purposely treading on his taxpayer's toes every time other taxpayers got on or off the taxpayers' bus. Then the angry taxpayer went and sat down in a seat for taxpayers which another taxpayer had just vacated. — Raymond Queneau, 'from Polyptotes' in Exercises in Style, 1945 (Eng. 1958)

definitions.example: I won't date Mary Jane again. I thought she was a stinker to leave before the end of the movie.

definitions.definition: Something difficult (e.g. a given puzzle) or unpleasant (e.g. negative review, nasty letter).
definitions.example: Today's crossword is a stinker.

definitions.definition: Something of poor quality.
Any of several species of large Antarctic petrels which feed on blubber and carrion and have an offensive odour, such as the giant fulmar.
A chemist.
A hot day.
To formulate a strategy.
A predetermined or prescribed set of rituals that are performed, usually by a religion.
An official worship service of the Christian church.
In Ancient Greece, a form of personal service to the state.
A laundering; a washing.
A place or room where laundering is done - including, by extension, other forms of laundering than clothes washing.
That which needs to be, is being, or has been laundered.
definitions.example: You've left your dirty laundry all over the house.

definitions.definition: The act of judging.
The power or faculty of performing such operations; especially, when unqualified, the faculty of judging or deciding rightly, justly, or wisely
definitions.example: a man of judgment

definitions.definition: The conclusion or result of judging; an opinion; a decision.
The act of determining, as in courts of law, what is conformable to law and justice; also, the determination, decision, or sentence of a court, or of a judge.
The final award; the last sentence.
To fill with wonder and surprise; to astonish, astound, surprise or perplex.
definitions.example: He was amazed when he found that the girl was a robot.

definitions.definition: To undergo amazement; to be astounded.
To stupefy; to knock unconscious.
To bewilder; to stupefy; to bring into a maze.
To terrify, to fill with panic.
Causing wonder and amazement; very surprising.
Possessing uniquely wonderful qualities.
Very good.
definitions.example: 2015, June 10, Lindsey Bever, "Morning Mix: Another reason seeing-eye dogs are amazing"http//www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2015/06/10/another-heroic-story-showing-why-seeing-eye-dogs-are-amazing/

definitions.definition: Heaven.
Something, especially a person's name in legal documents, that appears higher on the same page or on a preceding page.
Higher authority.
Betterment, raised status or condition.
Of heaven; heavenly.
(by ellipsis) Being located higher on the same page or on a preceding page.
Directly overhead; vertically on top of.
Higher in the same page; earlier in the order as far as writing products go.
Into or from heaven; in the sky.
definitions.example: He's in a better place now, floating free as the clouds above.

definitions.definition: In a higher place; upstairs; farther upstream.
Higher in rank, power, or position.
definitions.example: He appealed to the court above.

definitions.definition: In addition.
More in number.
Above zero; above freezing.
definitions.example: It was a cold day at only 5 above.

definitions.definition: On the upper half or the dorsal surface of an animal.
definitions.example: The sparrow I saw was rufous above and off-white below.

definitions.definition: Physically over; on top of; worn on top of, said of clothing.
definitions.example: He always put his coat on above his sweater.

definitions.definition: In or to a higher place; higher than; on or over the upper surface.
Farther north than.
definitions.example: Idaho is above Utah.

definitions.definition: Rising; appearing out of reach height-wise.

Higher than; superior to in any respect; surpassing; higher in measure, degree, volume, or pitch, etc. than; out of reach; not exposed to; not likely to be affected by; incapable of negative actions or thoughts.
definitions.example: Even the chief of police is not above suspicion.

definitions.definition: Higher in rank, status, or position.
definitions.example: to stand head and shoulders above the rest

definitions.definition: In addition to; besides.
definitions.example: above and beyond the call of duty

definitions.definition: Surpassing in number or quantity; more than.
definitions.example: That amount is way above our asking price.

definitions.definition: In preference to.
Too proud to stoop to; averse to; disinclined towards;
definitions.example: The owner was above taking more than a token salary.

definitions.definition: Beyond; on the other side.
Upstage of.
To arrange randomly; to make random
definitions.example: He randomised the marbles by spilling them onto the floor.

definitions.definition: (games) A game for a small child in which one covers one's face, then suddenly reveals it, saying "Boo!" or "Peekaboo!".
To peep out.
(of clothing) with holes, slits or transparent fabric to reveal what is normally hidden.
definitions.example: The model was wearing a peekaboo swimsuit.

definitions.definition: Boo (when showing oneself unexpectedly)
(of hair) Uncombed; dishevelled.
(by extension) Disorderly; untidy; messy; not kept up.
Rough; unpolished
Any vertical part of a structure, especially one of the goal posts in sports.
A word clued by the successive initial, middle, or final letters of the cross-lights in a double acrostic or triple acrostic.
An upright piano.
Short for upright vacuum cleaner.
To set upright or stand back up (something that has fallen).
Vertical; erect.
definitions.example: I was standing upright, waiting for my orders.

definitions.definition: Greater in height than breadth.
Of good morals; practicing ethical values.
(of a golf club) Having the head approximately at a right angle with the shaft.
In or into an upright position
